codeReview.summary: The implementation fully covers all required behaviors: ResultsPanel visibility gating, SymbolSelector dropdown, DataTable with sortable columns, pagination at 50 rows/page, column type indicators, empty state message, and CSV download. 33 tests pass with 0 TypeScript/lint errors. Two non-blocking issues were found: (1) the CSV download uses the original unsorted row order even when the user has sorted the table, and (2) the displayed row count label reads from data.size (backend-reported) rather than data.values.length (actual loaded rows), which could mislead if the backend ever sends partial data. Both are minor UX issues, not correctness bugs.

issues.description: CSV download calls symbolDataToCSV(data) which uses data.values (original order). If the user has sorted the table, the downloaded CSV will be in the original unsorted order, not matching the displayed order. The sorted values are available as `sortedValues` in the same closure. Fix: pass sortedValues to a version of symbolDataToCSV that accepts an overridden values array.

The toolbar displays '{data.size} row(s)' which reflects the backend-reported size field. However, pagination logic (totalPages, pageRows) is driven by sortedValues.length (i.e., data.values.length). If the backend ever returns a size field that doesn't match the actual values array length (e.g., a truncated preview with size indicating the total), the displayed count would contradict the actual pagination behavior. This is currently a cosmetic/documentation concern rather than a functional bug since the backend appears to always send full values, but it creates an implicit coupling assumption.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff documents a pre-existing bug where the visual query builder appends a trailing period to generated queries (e.g., 'ans(v, v1) :- destrieux(v, v1).'), which the backend parser rejects with UnexpectedTokenError. This backend parser quirk — that it expects Datalog queries WITHOUT a trailing period — is not documented in AGENTS.md or the architecture library. Any future worker modifying query submission or the visual builder would be unaware of this constraint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'The visual query builder generates queries with a trailing period... which causes UnexpectedTokenError from the backend parser. The backend parser expects queries without trailing period.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges mentions that CodeMirror 6's contenteditable element doesn't respond to standard agent-browser fill/keyboard-type commands reliably, requiring workarounds (using predicate browser clicks to build queries instead of typing). This tip is not in the frontend-worker SKILL.md, so future workers will repeat the same trial-and-error when verifying CodeMirror-based features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0]: 'The agent-browser verification was hampered by CodeMirror 6 internal state management - the cm-content contenteditable element doesn't respond to standard fill/keyboard-type commands reliably. Consider adding a tip in the skill that CodeMirror editors require special handling.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SymbolSelector dropdown is only rendered when symbolNames.length > 1 (i.e., hidden when there is exactly one symbol). The feature description says 'SymbolSelector: populated from query results keys'. It's ambiguous whether the selector should be shown even for a single symbol (for consistency/discoverability) or hidden. The current behavior (hidden for single symbol) is a reasonable UX choice but is not explicitly documented in AGENTS.md as a convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ResultsPanel.tsx line 414: '{symbolNames.length > 1 && (<SymbolSelector .../>)}' — selector hidden for single-symbol results.

summary: The results-table-display feature is well-implemented and passes all verification steps. All required behaviors are present: ResultsPanel visibility gating on executionStatus, SymbolSelector dropdown with default selection (last_parsed_symbol preferred), DataTable with sortable columns (asc/desc toggling with aria-sort), 50-row pagination with prev/next, column type indicators derived from row_type strings, empty state message, CSV download via Blob+URL.createObjectURL, and correct integration into Layout.tsx. 33 tests cover all required behaviors. Two non-blocking code quality issues were found: CSV download ignores current sort order, and row count label uses server-reported size rather than actual values array length. Three shared state observations are noted: the trailing-period backend parser quirk should be documented, the CodeMirror agent-browser limitation should be added to the skill, and the single-symbol selector hiding behavior could be documented.
